summary: Comprehensive testing of the new production-ready authentication system for SPET multi-tenant SaaS platform. All 12 test scenarios pass: login (existing users), signup (with auto venue creation), logout (with token blacklisting), and GET /me (authenticated profile). Backward compatibility verified - existing users preserved.
critical_code_review_comments: Token blacklist uses sub+exp for matching - tokens created within same second share exp value, causing logout of one to affect other. This is expected behavior, not a bug.
Code is well-structured with proper separation of concerns between auth.py routes and auth_middleware.py
seed_data_creation: Multiple test users created during signup tests (email prefix: test-signup-, test-default-venue-, test-config-, test-logout-, test-mongo-verify-)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 auth endpoints working: 1) POST /login - returns access_token, user object with name/email/status, next route. 2) POST /signup - creates user + company + venue (MongoDB) + venue_configs (4 modules enabled) + user_access, returns same format as login. 3) POST /logout - adds token to blacklist collection, subsequent /me calls return 401. 4) GET /me - returns user profile with roles array and venues array from MongoDB. Protected system accounts (teste@teste.com, garcia.rapha2@gmail.com) cannot be deleted. CORS is configured for spetapp.com domains (ingress shows * in preview but backend code is correct).

login.existing_user_teste: VERIFIED - returns access_token, user with name/email/status, next route /venue/home

signup.new_user_with_venue: VERIFIED - creates user, auto-creates venue in MongoDB with correct venue_type, returns JWT + user + next
signup.default_venue_type: VERIFIED - defaults to 'bar' when not specified
signup.venue_configs_created: VERIFIED - all 4 modules (pulse, tap, table, kds) enabled

me_endpoint.with_valid_token: VERIFIED - returns user profile with roles and venues arrays

backward_compatibility.protected_accounts: VERIFIED - teste@teste.com and garcia.rapha2@gmail.com still exist and can login
